INTRODUCTION In June, 1947, Kenneth Arnold, a Boise, Idaho, businessman and private pilot, publicly reported the now­famous sighting of a chainlike formation of disc­shaped objects near Mount Rainier, Washington. Resulting newspaper publicity of this incident caught the public interest, and, shortly thereafter, a rash of reports of unidentified aerial objects spawned the term "flying saucers".
